title: CLAUDE.md
text: Automerge document must “text” field type Automerge text. Shiny, editor content available input$<outputId>_content.

what: Why kanban boards work well for collaboration
title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: Collaborative text editing challenging users cursor position must preserved remote changes arrive. Standard HTML textareas can’t , leading frustrating experience. Kanban boards avoid problem action discrete: Adding item: Click button, type text, submit Moving item: Click arrow button Toggling item: Click checkbox Deleting item: Click delete button UI re-renders remote change, ’re middle anything gets disrupted. Automerge handles concurrent operations correctly: Two users add items simultaneously: items appear Two users move item: Last write wins One user deletes another moves: Delete wins

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: Open two browser windows try moving items columns.
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(   h3("Team Kanban"),   kanban_ui("board") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   kanban_server(     "board",     doc_id = "team-kanban",     initial_items = list(       todo = c("Design feature", "Write tests"),       in_progress = c("Code review"),       done = c("Deploy v1.0")     )   ) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(autoedit)  ui <- page_fillable(   padding = "1rem",   h2("Collaborative Kanban Board"),   p(class = "text-muted", "Session: ", textOutput("session_id", inline = TRUE)),   kanban_ui("board"),   hr(),   card(     card_header("Progress Summary"),     card_body(verbatimTextOutput("summary"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$session_id <- renderText(substr(session$token, 1, 6))    items <- kanban_server(     "board",     doc_id = "team-kanban",     initial_items = list(       todo = c("Design new feature", "Write tests", "Update docs"),       in_progress = c("Code review PR #42"),       done = c("Fix login bug", "Deploy v1.2")     )   )    output$summary <- renderPrint({     df <- items()     for (col in c("todo", "in_progress", "done")) {       col_items <- df[df$column == col, ]       cat(sprintf("%-12s %d items (%d done)\n",                   paste0(col, ":"),                   nrow(col_items),                   sum(col_items$done)))     }   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: can define columns:
code: kanban_ui(   "board",   columns = c(     backlog = "Backlog",     ready = "Ready",     doing = "Doing",     review = "Review",     done = "Done"   ),   column_colors = c(     backlog = "#64748b",     ready = "#8b5cf6",     doing = "#3b82f6",     review = "#f59e0b",     done = "#22c55e"   ) )  kanban_server(   "board",   columns = c(     backlog = "Backlog",     ready = "Ready",     doing = "Doing",     review = "Review",     done = "Done"   ),   initial_items = list(     backlog = c("Future feature A", "Future feature B"),     ready = c("Approved task"),     doing = c("Current work"),     review = c("PR #123"),     done = c("Shipped!")   ) )

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: doc_id parameter identifies collaborative document. sessions using doc_id synchronize together:
code: # These sync together kanban_server("board1", doc_id = "shared-board") kanban_server("board2", doc_id = "shared-board")  # This is independent kanban_server("board3", doc_id = "other-board")

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: kanban_server() returns reactive data frame columns id, text, done, column:
code: items <- kanban_server("board", doc_id = "my-board")  observe({   df <- items()   done_count <- sum(df$column == "done")   total <- nrow(df)   message(sprintf("%d/%d items done", done_count, total)) })

title: Collaborative Kanban Board
text: kanban module: syncs within single R process (multi-process scaling) Stores documents memory (persistence across restarts) production deployments needing persistence scaling, use editor() sync server.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: run examples, need autoedit package installed: Open multiple browser windows tabs pointing Shiny app URL see real-time collaboration action.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("shikokuchuo/autoedit")

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: editor() widget provides best collaborative editing experience. uses CodeMirror 6 automerge-codemirror integration, applies Automerge operations directly CodeMirror’s document model. means cursor stays place others edit, get full syntax highlighting, documents can saved restored across sessions.

title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: following example uses autosync package, provides -process Automerge sync server:
code:
library(shiny) library(bslib) library(autoedit) library(autosync) library(automerge)  # Room-specific initial content templates room_templates <- list(   standup = "# Daily Standup

## What I did yesterday
- 

## What I'm doing today
- 

## Blockers
- 
",   planning = "# Sprint Planning

## Goals
- 

## User Stories
- 

## Capacity
- 
",   retrospective = "# Retrospective

## What went well
- 

## What could be improved
- 

## Action items
- 
",   brainstorm = "# Brainstorming Session

## Ideas
- 

## Discussion Notes
- 
" )  # Create and start the sync server (once, at app startup) sync_server <- amsync_server() sync_server$start()  # Create a document for each room with initial content doc_ids <- list() for (room in names(room_templates)) {   doc_ids[[room]] <- create_document(sync_server)   doc <- get_document(sync_server, doc_ids[[room]])   am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text(room_templates[[room]]))   am_commit(doc, "init") }  ui <- page_fillable(   padding = "1rem",   div(class = "d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3",     h2("Collaborative Meeting Notes", class = "mb-0"),     downloadButton("export", "Export as Quarto", class = "btn-sm")   ),   navset_card_pill(     id = "room",     nav_panel("Daily Standup", value = "standup", uiOutput("editor_standup")),     nav_panel("Sprint Planning", value = "planning", uiOutput("editor_planning")),     nav_panel("Retrospective", value = "retrospective", uiOutput("editor_retrospective")),     nav_panel("Brainstorming", value = "brainstorm", uiOutput("editor_brainstorm"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor_standup <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_standup", height = "500px"))   output$editor_planning <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_planning", height = "500px"))   output$editor_retrospective <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_retrospective", height = "500px"))   output$editor_brainstorm <- renderUI(editor_output("ed_brainstorm", height = "500px"))    output$ed_standup <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["standup"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_planning <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["planning"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_retrospective <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["retrospective"]], height = "500px"))   output$ed_brainstorm <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_ids[["brainstorm"]], height = "500px"))    current_content <- reactive({     switch(input$room,       "standup" = input$ed_standup_content,       "planning" = input$ed_planning_content,       "retrospective" = input$ed_retrospective_content,       "brainstorm" = input$ed_brainstorm_content     )   })    output$export <- downloadHandler(     filename = function() paste0(input$room, "-notes-", Sys.Date(), ".qmd"),     content = function(file) {       titles <- c(standup = "Daily Standup", planning = "Sprint Planning",                   retrospective = "Retrospective", brainstorm = "Brainstorming")       front_matter <- sprintf("---
title: "%s"
date: "%s"
format: html
---

",                               titles[input$room], Sys.Date())       writeLines(paste0(front_matter, current_content()), file)     }   ) }  # Clean up when app stops onStop(function() sync_server$close())  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Why not use a standard textarea?
title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: might wonder whether simpler approach using Shiny’s textAreaInput() work collaborative editing. autoedit package include textarea_ui() textarea_server() functions synchronize text using Automerge without requiring sync server. However, approach fundamental limitation makes unsuitable real-time collaboration.

previous_headings: Why not use a standard textarea?
title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: HTML <textarea> elements API granular text updates. way update textarea programmatically replace entire .value property. means another user’s changes arrive, cursor position lost. can demonstrate problem following app: click button, cursor jumps end text. real collaborative scenario, happen every time another user makes edit, making impossible type continuously.
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(   h3("Cursor Reset Demo"),   p("1. Click in the middle of the text below"),   p("2. Click 'Simulate Remote Edit' - watch your cursor jump"),   textarea_ui("notes", height = "200px"),    actionButton("remote_edit", "Simulate Remote Edit") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   textarea_server(     "notes",     doc_id = "cursor-test",     initial_text = "Place your cursor HERE in the middle then click the button."   )    # Directly modify the master doc to simulate another user's edit   observeEvent(input$remote_edit, {     master <- autoedit:::.master_docs[["cursor-test"]]     if (!is.null(master)) {       text_obj <- automerge::am_get(master$doc, automerge::AM_ROOT, "text")       content <- automerge::am_text_content(text_obj)       # Append text to simulate remote edit       automerge::am_text_splice(text_obj, nchar(content), 0L, " [REMOTE EDIT]")       automerge::am_commit(master$doc, "remote edit")       master$version <- master$version + 1L     }   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

previous_headings: Why not use a standard textarea?
title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: editor() widget uses CodeMirror 6 automerge-codemirror integration. CodeMirror maintains proper document model can apply operations incrementally. remote change arrives, CodeMirror: Applies change correct position Adjusts cursor selection positions account change Preserves editing context editor() sync server recommended approach collaborative text editing.

previous_headings: Why not use a standard textarea?
title: Collaborative Meeting Notes App
text: need serverless collaboration without cursor position issues, consider using discrete UI components instead free-form text. example, collaborative kanban board works well action (add item, move item, toggle, delete) atomic - ’s cursor position preserve. See “Collaborative Kanban Board” vignette details.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: standard Shiny app gives user independent session. User ’s slider connection User B’s slider. sync_inputs() changes storing every input value shared Automerge document. one user changes control, new value propagated sessions automatically. works well app inputs standard Shiny widgets (sliders, dropdowns, numeric inputs, checkboxes, radio buttons, text inputs) goal shared view output.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: single line server function makes app collaborative: Open two browser tabs pointing app. Move slider one tab — tab’s slider moves match plots update together.
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Collaborative Distribution Viewer"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput("dist", "Distribution",         c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),       sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),       checkboxInput("density", "Show density curve", FALSE)     ),     mainPanel(       plotOutput("plot")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   sync_inputs()    output$plot <- renderPlot({     data <- switch(input$dist,       Normal = rnorm(input$n),       Uniform = runif(input$n),       Exponential = rexp(input$n)     )     hist(data, main = input$dist, col = "steelblue", border = "white",          probability = input$density)     if (input$density) lines(density(data), col = "red", lwd = 2)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: sync_inputs() sets three things current session: shared Automerge document — entire input state stored flat map: {"xcol": "Sepal.Length", "ycol": "Sepal.Width", "clusters": 3}. master copy lives package-level environment shared sessions R process. session maintains local copy syncs incrementally master using Automerge’s sync protocol. observer local changes — Watches reactiveValuesToList(input) changes. user moves slider, new value written local Automerge document synced master. reactive version counter notifies sessions. handler remote changes — master version bumps (another session changed something), local document syncs, reads new values, pushes browser. JavaScript message handler calls Shiny’s input binding receiveMessage() method update widget — mechanism updateSliderInput() etc. use internally. Feedback loops suppressed tracking known values: remote update sets input new value, resulting echo browser recognised skipped.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: default, scalar inputs (string, numeric, logical) synchronized. Inputs starting . (Shiny internals) always excluded. Use include sync specific inputs: Use exclude sync everything except certain inputs: Action buttons (whose values integer click counters) scalar-typed way sync_inputs() checks, generally excluded automatically. find input synced shouldn’t , use exclude.
code: # Only sync these two controls sync_inputs(include = c("xcol", "ycol")) # Sync all inputs except the action button sync_inputs(exclude = "reset_btn")

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: doc_id parameter identifies shared state. sessions using doc_id see inputs:
code: # These sessions share state sync_inputs(doc_id = "classroom-demo")  # This session is independent sync_inputs(doc_id = "instructor-view")

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: default, shared state lives memory lost R restarts. Pass path save Automerge document disk automatically: document saved every change (223 bytes typical 3-input app) reloaded next startup. Users reconnect pick exactly left . works Automerge document self-contained — holds every input value single binary blob via am_save() / am_load().
code: sync_inputs(path = "app-state.automerge")

what: When to use sync_inputs() vs other approaches
title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: sync_inputs() designed common case group people want look dashboard anyone able drive controls. treats input atomic value last-write-wins semantics, appropriate controls like sliders dropdowns “merging” two values meaningful.

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: sync_inputs() records every input change Automerge commit descriptive message timestamp. replay module lets step history, reconstructing exact app state point. Add replay_ui() UI replay_server() server: timeline slider shows one position per meaningful change (init commits new sessions filtered ). Dragging slider reconstructs document step pushes values widgets, plot updates match. slider latest step, live syncing paused — moving slider end resumes normal operation. step buttons (first, previous, next, last) navigate one commit time. play button animates history automatically.
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Collaborative Distribution Viewer"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       selectInput("dist", "Distribution",         c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),       sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100)     ),     mainPanel(       plotOutput("plot"),       replay_ui("timeline")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   replaying <- sync_inputs()   replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying)    output$plot <- renderPlot({     data <- switch(input$dist,       Normal = rnorm(input$n),       Uniform = runif(input$n),       Exponential = rexp(input$n)     )     hist(data, main = input$dist, col = "steelblue", border = "white")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: replay_ui() accepts two styling parameters: show_messages — Display commit message step (default TRUE). Messages describe changed, e.g. "dist: Exponential" "n: 250". playback_ms — Milliseconds steps animated playback (default 1000). Pass matching values replay_server():
code: replay_ui("timeline", show_messages = FALSE, playback_ms = 500) replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying,               show_messages = FALSE, playback_ms = 500)

title: Collaborative Shiny Apps
text: Single R process — shared state lives memory. multi-process deployments (e.g., multiple Shiny Server workers), use external sync server editor() instead. Scalar values — Complex inputs like file uploads, data table selections, plot brush events synchronized. length-1 character, numeric, logical values synced.

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: editor provides best experience collaborative text editing, proper cursor preservation remote changes arrive. sync_inputs makes entire Shiny app collaborative single function call. scalar inputs (sliders, dropdowns, checkboxes, text inputs) synchronized automatically. Pair replay_ui() / replay_server() step full history input changes. kanban module works well without sync server actions (add, toggle, delete, move) discrete - ’s cursor position preserve. textarea syncs correctly UX limitations (cursor jumps remote edits), making suitable simple use cases.

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(kanban_ui("board"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   kanban_server("board", initial_items = list(     todo = c("Design feature", "Write tests"),     in_progress = c("Code review"),     done = c("Deploy v1.0")   )) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit)  ui <- fluidPage(   selectInput("dist", "Distribution", c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),   sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),   plotOutput("plot"),   replay_ui("timeline") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   replaying <- sync_inputs()   replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying)    output$plot <- renderPlot({     data <- switch(input$dist,       Normal = rnorm(input$n),       Uniform = runif(input$n),       Exponential = rexp(input$n)     )     hist(data, main = input$dist, col = "steelblue", border = "white")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Collaborative Text Editing for Shiny with Automerge
text: Open either app multiple browser windows real-time collaboration.
code: library(shiny) library(autoedit) library(autosync) library(automerge)  sync_server <- amsync_server() sync_server$start()  doc_id <- create_document(sync_server) doc <- get_document(sync_server, doc_id) am_put(doc, AM_ROOT, "text", am_text("Start typing...")) am_commit(doc, "init")  ui <- fluidPage(editor_output("editor"))  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$editor <- editor_render(editor(sync_server$url, doc_id)) }  onStop(function() sync_server$close()) shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: Output render functions using editor within Shiny applications interactive R Markdown documents.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
code: editor_output(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  editor_render(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: outputId Output variable read . width, height Widget dimensions (must valid CSS unit number coerced string "px" appended). expr expression generates editor widget. env environment evaluate expr. quoted Logical, whether expr quoted expression.

title: Shiny bindings for editor — editor-shiny
text: editor_output() returns Shiny output element. editor_render() returns Shiny render function.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: Creates real-time collaborative text editor using CodeMirror 6 Automerge CRDT, synchronized via WebSocket automerge-repo compatible sync server.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
code: editor(server_url, doc_id, timeout = 10000, width = "100%", height = "400px")

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: server_url WebSocket URL (ws:// wss://) sync server. doc_id Document ID (base58check encoded). timeout Connection timeout milliseconds. Default 10000. width, height Widget dimensions.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: htmlwidget object.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
text: widget connects specified sync server synchronizes CodeMirror 6 editor Automerge document. Multiple users can edit document simultaneously automatic conflict resolution. Automerge document must "text" field type Automerge text (created automerge::am_text()). Shiny applications, current editor content available input value input$<outputId>_content.

title: Collaborative text editor widget — editor
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Connect to the public Automerge sync server editor("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id")  # Or use with an autosync server server <- autosync::amsync_server(port = 3030) server$start()  doc_id <- autosync::create_document(server) doc <- autosync::get_document(server, doc_id) automerge::am_put(doc, automerge::AM_ROOT, "text", automerge::am_text("")) automerge::am_commit(doc, "init")  editor(server$url, doc_id) } # }

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
code: kanban_server(   id,   doc_id = "default",   columns = c(todo = "To Do", in_progress = "In Progress", done = "Done"),   initial_items = NULL )

title: Collaborative kanban board server — kanban_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used kanban_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. kanban boards doc_id synchronize together. Default "default". columns Named character vector defining columns (must match UI). initial_items Optional named list initial items per column. Names column IDs, values character vectors item texts.

code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(kanban_ui("board"))   server <- function(input, output, session) {     kanban_server("board", initial_items = list(       todo = c("Design feature", "Write tests"),       in_progress = c("Code review"),       done = c("Deploy v1.0")     ))   }   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative kanban board UI — kanban_ui
text: id Module ID. columns Named character vector defining columns. Names internal IDs, values display labels. Default c(todo = "", in_progress = "Progress", done = "Done"). column_colors Named character vector CSS colors column headers. Names match column IDs. Default provides red/yellow/green styling.

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
text: Server logic replay timeline module. Reads change history master Automerge document created sync_inputs(), reconstructs snapshots step, pushes values widgets.

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
code: replay_server(   id,   doc_id = "default",   replaying,   show_messages = TRUE,   playback_ms = 1000 )

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used replay_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. Must match doc_id used sync_inputs(). Default "default". replaying reactiveVal returned sync_inputs(). show_messages Whether display commit messages. Default TRUE. playback_ms Interval milliseconds steps animated playback. Default 1000.

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
text: Called side effects. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Replay timeline server — replay_server
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(     shiny::selectInput("dist", "Distribution",       c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),     shiny::sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),     shiny::plotOutput("plot"),     replay_ui("timeline")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     replaying <- sync_inputs()     replay_server("timeline", replaying = replaying)     output$plot <- shiny::renderPlot(       hist(switch(input$dist,         Normal = rnorm(input$n),         Uniform = runif(input$n),         Exponential = rexp(input$n)       ))     )   }   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

what: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: Makes Shiny inputs collaborative synchronizing values across connected sessions using Automerge CRDT, without requiring external sync server.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
code: sync_inputs(   session = shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain(),   doc_id = "default",   include = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   path = NULL )

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: session Shiny session object. Default uses shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain(). doc_id Document identifier string. sessions using doc_id share synchronized input state. Default "default". include Optional character vector input IDs synchronize. NULL (default), eligible inputs synchronized. exclude Optional character vector input IDs exclude synchronization. Applied include. path Optional file path persistent state. provided, Automerge document saved file every change reloaded automatically app restarts. Set NULL (default) -memory .

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: reactiveVal (logical). FALSE normal operation, TRUE replay. Can ignored replay used. Called primarily side effects.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: Call function Shiny server logic enable collaborative input synchronization. Scalar string, numeric, logical input values synchronized automatically. Complex inputs (file uploads, action buttons, data table selections, etc.) excluded default. user changes input, new value propagated connected sessions. entire app state stored single Automerge document, providing automatic conflict resolution via CRDT. Inputs names starting "." (Shiny internal inputs) always excluded.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: path provided, shared document saved disk (via automerge::am_save()) every change. next app startup, document loaded disk sessions resume saved state. makes shared input state survive R process restarts.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
text: function synchronizes sessions within single R process. multi-process deployments, use external sync server editor() widget instead.

title: Synchronize Shiny inputs across sessions — sync_inputs
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(     shiny::selectInput("dist", "Distribution",       c("Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential")),     shiny::sliderInput("n", "Observations", 10, 500, 100),     shiny::plotOutput("plot")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     sync_inputs()     output$plot <- shiny::renderPlot(       hist(switch(input$dist,         Normal = rnorm(input$n),         Uniform = runif(input$n),         Exponential = rexp(input$n)       ))     )   }   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: Server logic collaborative textarea synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
code: textarea_server(id, doc_id = "default", initial_text = "", debounce_ms = 150)

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: id Module ID (must match ID used textarea_ui()). doc_id Document identifier. textareas doc_id synchronize together. Default "default". initial_text Initial text content. used creating new document; ignored document already exists. debounce_ms Debounce delay milliseconds text input changes. Default 150ms.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: reactive expression returning current synchronized text.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
text: module uses Shiny's reactive system synchronize text across multiple browser sessions without requiring external sync server. session maintains local Automerge document syncs shared master document using Automerge's sync protocol. Concurrent edits automatically merged using Automerge's CRDT algorithm, ensuring eventual consistency across sessions.

title: Collaborative textarea server — textarea_server
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Creates textarea input synchronizes across Shiny sessions using Automerge CRDT, without requiring external sync server.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: textarea_ui(   id,   label = NULL,   width = "100%",   height = "200px",   placeholder = NULL )

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: id Module ID. label Label textarea, NULL label. width width input (e.g., "100%", "400px"). height height input (e.g., "200px"). placeholder Placeholder text empty.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
text: Shiny UI element.

title: Collaborative textarea UI — textarea_ui
code: if (interactive()) {   ui <- shiny::fluidPage(textarea_ui("editor"))   server <- function(input, output, session) textarea_server("editor")   shiny::shinyApp(ui, server) }

text: Initial release.
